parameters.content:
### Daily Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — daily at 6:00. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Keep Valid Emails

### Keep Valid Emails

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Score the Lead

### Score the Lead

Custom JavaScript: Score each email 0-100 from simple heuristics

Next: Assign Round-Robin Owner

### Assign Round-Robin Owner

Custom JavaScript: Round-robin owner assignment

Next: Route by Heat

### Route by Heat

Routes each item to one of 3 lanes: hot, warm + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Hot → Notion + Instant Ping · ②→ Warm → Drip via Gmail · ③→ Cold → Park in Nurture List

### Hot → Notion + Instant Ping

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Ports: ①→ Notify Assigned Owner · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Notify Assigned Owner

Posts a message to Slack — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: ①→ Combine Lead Paths · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Warm → Drip via Gmail

Sends an email via Gmail — subject is the first line of the message body, recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

Ports: ①→ Combine Lead Paths · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Cold → Park in Nurture List

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Ports: ①→ Combine Lead Paths · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

nodes.name: Note: Cold → Park in Nurture List

parameters.content:
### Combine Lead Paths

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Cap Batch Volume

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 100 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Guard Non-Empty from

### Guard Non-Empty from

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Notion)

### Append Audit Row (Notion)

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#inbox-ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Gmail

### Escalate Failure to Gmail

Sends an email via Gmail — subject is the first line of the message body, recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2

# Follow up with trial users with Notion and Gmail at scale

**Pattern:** Drip Sequence · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Follow up with trial users with Notion and Gmail at scale. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual inbox triage is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Daily Schedule → Keep Valid Emails → Score the Lead → Assign Round-Robin Owner → Route by Heat → Hot → Notion + Instant Ping → Notify Assigned Owner → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to drip sequence without manual effort
- Anyone automating inbox triage in marketing
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Responds in seconds
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Scales without extra headcount

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2; Notion integration token; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Round-robin owner assignment happens right after scoring, before routing — every lead gets an accountable owner regardless of tier, so even a 'cold' lead parked in the nurture list is someone's responsibility later, not an anonymous row in a spreadsheet.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered — push further:

1. Change the hot/warm threshold values and watch the tier distribution shift.
2. Add a 4th owner to the round-robin pool.
3. Add a follow-up wait+check to the warm-drip branch, similar to the drip-ladder spine.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your inbox triage process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
